module_microarchitecture.name: sram_mbist_demo_controller
module_microarchitecture.implementation_style: Single-clock MMIO controller with explicit SRAM intent signals, register-file style state storage, and simple BIST/IRQ latching.
module_microarchitecture.control_strategy: Use synchronous decode of wr_en/wr_addr/wr_data and rd_en/rd_addr to update and read the fixed register map. CONTROL.ENABLE gates normal firmware access, CONTROL.SOFT_RESET self-clears after being sampled, and CONTROL.IRQ_ENABLE qualifies irq generation. MEM_CONTROL.MEM_WRITE and MEM_CONTROL.MEM_READ are treated as one-cycle request strobes to the SRAM path. bist_start and BIST_CONTROL.START are ORed as a single BIST request intent, with BIST_CONTROL.CLEAR_RESULT and IRQ_CLEAR clearing latched status bits.
module_microarchitecture.state_strategy: Maintain a small set of synchronous registers for CONTROL, MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, MEM_RDATA, MEM_CONTROL, BIST_CONTROL, BIST_STATUS, IRQ_STATUS, and internal ready/busy/running/done/fail indicators. Last failing BIST address is held in a 6-bit field. Reset clears all software-visible state to zero and leaves the SRAM path inactive/non-writing.
module_microarchitecture.output_strategy: Drive rd_data from the selected register readback value, with MEM_RDATA reflecting the last observed SRAM read data or current captured read result. ready indicates idle/accepting state outside reset and outside BIST busy. bist_done and bist_fail reflect the latched BIST status, and irq asserts only when IRQ_ENABLE is set and a done/fail cause is latched. sram_clk, sram_csb, sram_web, sram_addr, and sram_din expose explicit memory intent for downstream macro binding or inference.
ownership_notes: Top-level owns only register/state orchestration and memory access intent; SRAM storage itself is owned by the selected memory implementation.
The top module is responsible for preserving OpenRAM-compatible memory intent and MBIST collateral visibility.

module_microarchitecture.name: demo_sram_32x64
module_microarchitecture.implementation_style: Canonical OpenRAM-compatible single-port SRAM abstraction with synchronous control and a minimal macro-facing interface.
module_microarchitecture.control_strategy: Treat csb as active-low select and web as active-low write enable. The controller is expected to provide synchronous clk, address, and data intent on the same clock domain.
module_microarchitecture.state_strategy: Model or macro holds 64 words by 32 bits. No explicit reset state is defined; contents are implementation-defined unless the selected collateral initializes them.
module_microarchitecture.output_strategy: Provide synchronous dout behavior consistent with a single-port SRAM interface so synthesis and DFT tools can recognize a memory-like structure.
ownership_notes: This module is the intended binding target when OpenRAM collateral exists.
The module name is reserved to preserve memory macro replacement compatibility.

module_microarchitecture.name: demo_sram_32x64_model
module_microarchitecture.implementation_style: Synthesizable fallback single-port memory wrapper with the same interface and naming intent as the macro abstraction.
module_microarchitecture.control_strategy: Use the same synchronous csb/web/address/data convention as the macro-facing SRAM contract, avoiding vendor-specific primitives.
module_microarchitecture.state_strategy: Store 64 x 32 words in synthesizable memory logic for simulation and synthesis continuity when macro collateral is absent.
module_microarchitecture.output_strategy: Return dout from the selected memory location under synchronous read behavior compatible with the macro contract.
ownership_notes: This is the fallback implementation path only.
It preserves memory intent for downstream synthesis and MBIST collateral generation.

fsm_intent.description: No explicit complex FSM is required by the specification. The implementation intent is a small synchronous register-file/controller with simple request qualification and status latching, not a multi-state protocol machine.

output_mapping: rd_data is a muxed readback of the selected register address
ready reflects idle/accepting status and deasserts during reset or busy BIST activity
bist_done and bist_fail reflect latched BIST result state
irq is the AND of IRQ_ENABLE with latched IRQ_STATUS causes
sram_clk mirrors clk as the memory intent clock
sram_csb, sram_web, sram_addr, and sram_din are generated from memory request sequencing and staged memory registers

internal_storage: 64 x 32 memory array or macro-backed storage
output_mapping: dout returns synchronous read data from the addressed word
dout returns synchronous read data from the addressed word

sequencing_notes: Reset is synchronous to clk and clears control/status/IRQ-visible state and software-visible read data to zero.
Firmware MMIO writes update the addressed register on the sampled write strobe; read data is returned through a simple address decode on rd_en.
MEM_ADDR and MEM_WDATA stage memory access operands before MEM_CONTROL requests act on them.
MEM_CONTROL.MEM_WRITE and MEM_CONTROL.MEM_READ are one-cycle request intents; the implementation should keep the memory path simple and synchronous.
bist_start and BIST_CONTROL.START are logically combined into a single BIST request intent.
If BIST and normal memory activity are requested together, BIST request intent takes priority over normal memory operations.
BIST_CONTROL.CLEAR_RESULT and IRQ_CLEAR clear the latched result/IRQ state without altering the memory contents.
IRQ_STATUS is latch-based and remains set until cleared by software action.
ready should reflect an idle state after reset release when no BIST activity is in progress.
timing_notes: Single synchronous clock domain only; all control and memory intent is aligned to clk.
Operating point is 50 MHz with a 20 ns nominal period.
reset_n is synchronous active-low; deassertion behavior is sampled on clk.
The SRAM abstraction is driven as a synchronous single-port memory on the same clock domain.
Write strobes are treated as one-cycle sampled requests unless the register contract explicitly indicates self-clear behavior.
Keep the fallback memory wrapper synthesizable and free of vendor-only primitives.
consistency_notes: No hierarchy, ports, or filenames may differ from digital_spec_json.
